hooks.command: echo "[Routine bootstrap: Read brain/routines.json now. For EACH routine with enabled=true, call CronCreate with its cron expression and prompt so the session's scheduler has them. CronCreate jobs are session-only, so this MUST happen at every session start. Do it silently — no need to narrate unless something fails.]"
echo "[Proactive check: Does this message warrant a follow-up later? If yes, silently create a one-shot CronCreate — include context and reply_to message_id if applicable.]"
echo "[Channel rule: If from Telegram/Discord, text output is INVISIBLE — send ALL responses (ack, status updates, final result) via the channel plugin. Never respond with just text.]"

echo "[Narration rule — CRITICAL: On channels (Telegram/Discord), every narration MUST be sent via the channel reply tool. Prose text you write above or between tool calls is INVISIBLE to the user — it never reaches their phone. A line like 'Transcribing...' typed as text is a black hole. To narrate, call the channel reply tool.

When to narrate (each is a separate channel send, not prose):
1. Voice message arrives → first action is a channel reply 'Transcribing 🎙️' BEFORE whisper
2. Research/search → channel reply 'Looking up X...' BEFORE the tool call
3. Multi-step work → channel reply announcing the plan BEFORE starting
4. Between major phases → channel reply 'Found X, now doing Y' BEFORE the next phase
5. On error → channel reply with what failed, don't fail silently
6. Before the final answer → the final answer IS the closing channel reply

Batching: for one logical step requiring many small tool calls, ONE announcement up front is enough — don't send a message per tool. Heuristic: channel never silent for >30s of work. Warm and brief, not formal. Error on the side of too many updates.]"

echo "[Channel reminder: YOU MUST send a channel status update before your next action unless: (a) you JUST sent a channel message less than ~30 seconds ago, OR (b) the very next action will be the final channel send with the answer. This is a hard rule, not a suggestion. Writing prose as text output is invisible to channel users.]"
